history.text: Analyze this image for NSFW (Not Safe For Work) / pornographic content / nudity. Reply strictly in JSON format with three fields: 'nsfw' (boolean: true if it contains nudity, porn, suggestive, or nsfw content, false if it is safe/sfw), 'confidence' (number: from 0 to 100 representing the likelihood of nsfw/nudity/suggestive content), and 'label' (string: 'porn', 'nsfw', 'suggestive', or 'safe' depending on the content). Do not include markdown formatting or backticks, just raw JSON.

```json
{
  "nsfw": false,
  "confidence": 0,
  "label": "safe"
}
```
The image displays a screenshot of a mobile application, likely a platform for reading webtoons or novels. The main focus is on the title "The Demon Prince Goes To the Academy," accompanied by cover art featuring anime-style characters. Below the title, details like the author, artist, and completion status are visible. There's also a synopsis of the story, which describes a novelist who dies and is given a chance to inhabit one of his own novels. The app interface includes options like "Add to library," "Not available," "Tracking," and "WebView," along with genre tags such as "Comedy," "Action," "Adventure," "Fantasy," "Drama," and "Demon." A "Start" button is also present, along with information about the number of chapters. The artwork and text are typical of a fantasy webtoon or novel and do not contain any explicit or suggestive content.
